prompt: I have psychophysics data from a temporal order judgment (TOJ) experiment. The data has columns: SOA (stimulus onset asynchrony in ms, ranging from -200 to +200), response (proportion 'audio first' responses), and condition (visual_lead, audio_lead). Plot a psychometric function with sigmoid fits for each condition, mark the PSE (point of subjective equality), and make it publication-quality for Journal of Neuroscience submission.
expected_output_criteria: Uses matplotlib for publication-quality static figure
Sigmoid/logistic fit to psychometric data
PSE marked with vertical dashed lines
JNeurosci style: specific font sizes, DPI >= 300, appropriate figure size
Two conditions plotted with distinct colors from a colorblind-safe palette
Proper axis labels: SOA (ms), Proportion 'Audio First'
Legend with condition labels
Code uses synthetic data for demonstration

prompt: Create an interactive brain activation heatmap from my fMRI GLM results. I have a CSV with columns: ROI_name, x, y, z (MNI coordinates), t_statistic, p_value, cluster_size. Show activations color-coded by t-statistic with hover tooltips showing all info. Use Plotly for interactivity.
expected_output_criteria: Uses Plotly for interactive HTML output
Scatter3d or surface plot of MNI coordinates
Color scale mapped to t-statistic values
Hover tooltips with ROI name, coordinates, t-stat, p-value, cluster size
Appropriate neuroscience color scheme (hot/cool for positive/negative)
Interactive rotation and zoom
Title and axis labels in MNI space

prompt: Make a spike raster plot with PSTH (peri-stimulus time histogram) below it for 50 neurons recorded during a visual working memory delay period. The stimulus appears at t=0, the delay period is 0-1000ms, and the probe appears at t=1000ms. Mark these events on the plot. Show firing rate changes across the population.
expected_output_criteria: Two-panel figure: raster on top, PSTH below
Shared x-axis (time in ms)
Raster: individual spike ticks for 50 neurons
PSTH: mean firing rate with SEM shading
Event markers: stimulus onset (0ms), probe (1000ms)
Synthetic spike data generated for demonstration
Publication-quality formatting
Uses matplotlib subplot with appropriate aspect ratios
